div.p: Quality vs Diversity Trade-offs in LLM Decoders
"The cat sat on the" Token IDs: [1, 5, 3, 7, 2]

ul.li: Greedy: Fast but myopic — commits to local maxima, no error recovery.
Beam Search: Balanced — explores k paths, finds better sequences but O(k*n) cost.
Sampling: Creative but unpredictable — explores full distribution, may produce nonsense.
T → 0: Distribution sharpens; approaches Greedy behavior.
T → ∞: Distribution flattens; approaches Uniform Random (Max Entropy).

div.p: Selected: "mat" (highest) New context: "The cat sat on the mat"
"The cat sat on the mat." Deterministic, safe, common
"The cat sat on the mat." Explored 9 paths, chose global best
New context: "The cat sat on the couch" Next: stochastic roll-out
"The cat sat on the couch quietly." Diverse, creative, non-deterministic

Logic Consistency 🥇 Beam Search 🥈 Greedy
🥉 Sampling
Novelty / Surprise 🥇 Sampling 🥈 Beam Search 🥉 Greedy
Computational Cost 🥇 Greedy 🥈 Sampling 🥉 Beam Search
Application Type: Greedy → Prod, Summary Beam → Translation Sampling → Creative Chat
